name: Irish Soda Bread with Herbs
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 325°F.
2. In a bowl fitted with a paddle, combine flour, chopped herbs, baking powder, salt, and baking soda (breaking up any lumps). Mix on low speed for 2 minutes.
3. Combine buttermilk and vegetable oil in a stainless steel bowl and whisk gently to combine.
4. Gradually add the buttermilk mixture to the dry ingredients while mixing on low speed until a dough forms, about 30-40 seconds.
5. Scrape the dough onto a floured surface and knead with your hands for an additional 30 seconds.
6. Form the dough into a round loaf and pinch a small handful of dough together. Twist and place the loaf, pinched side down, on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.
7. Whisk egg yolk with water and lightly brush over the top of the loaf. Cut a shallow X into the top.
8. Bake for 50-55 minutes until a hollow sound is heard when tapped. Cool to room temperature before slicing.
Variations:
Scallion Soda Bread: Use scallions instead of herbs.
Raisin Soda Bread: Use raisins instead of herbs.
Caraway Seed Soda Bread: Use caraway seeds instead of herbs.
